title: Digital ID - Have your say | Robbie Moore MP
meta_description: On Monday 8 December, I will be leading the debate AGAINST the rollout of mandatory digital ID.The official parliamentary petition has garnered nearly 3 million signatures across the UK.
headings: h3: Robbie Moore MP Delivering your priorities for Keighley and Ilkley
text_excerpt: Digital ID - Have your say | Robbie Moore MP Skip to main content Robbie Moore MP Main navigation Home Campaigns Keighley & Ilkley News 🏥 New Airedale Hospital SECURED Contact 🔍 Search Digital ID - Have your say Stop The Scouts Tax - Sign the Open Letter ✍️ Worth Valley By-Election - What you need to know 🚜 Stop the Family Farm Tax 🚨 Stop the Grooming Gangs Robbie Moore MP Digital ID - Have your say Tweet On Monday 8 December, I will be leading the debate AGAINST the rollout of mandatory digital ID. The official parliamentary petition has garnered nearly 3 million signatures across the UK. Now, I'd like to hear your thoughts as I prepare for the debate. If you would like to share your thoughts or experiences on this issue and/or you would be happy for me to share these in Parliament, please do fill out my survey below: Digital ID Survey Current Your details Leave this field blank Do you support the rollout of mandatory Digital ID? Yes No Other [Optional] Why? Share your thoughts or experiences Are you happy for me to share your experiences in my speech in Parliament? Yes No Yes, but anonymously In order to make this survey representative, how did you vote in the 2024 General Election? Labour Lib Dem Conservative Green Reform Independent Rather not say Did not vote This question contains special category data relating to your political opinion that may be shared with the wider Conservative Party for the purposes of Democratic Engagement with your permission. Please only answer this question if you consent to the processing of this data and sharing it with Conservative Party in accordance with our Data Protection and Privacy policy . If you do not answer this question then this data will not be shared. If a General Election were to be called tomorrow, what are the chances of you voting for each of the following parties?
